hook.text: Screening finds a small tumor that would never have caused symptoms. You get surgery anyway. Were you helped?

buildup.text: Overdiagnosis means detecting a real condition that would never have harmed the patient.

discovery.text: Thyroid, breast, and prostate cancer screening all have significant overdiagnosis rates.

twist.text: The patient feels saved. But they suffered treatment side effects for a cancer that wouldn't have mattered.

climax.text: Overdiagnosis is invisible: you can't know which individual was overdiagnosed. It only shows up in population data.

punchline.text: Finding more isn't always helping more.

quiz.question: What is overdiagnosis?
options: Detecting a real condition that would never have caused harm if left undiscovered
Using too many diagnostic tests on one patient
